howItWorks.intro: This muscle recovery time calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Recovery Time
rules.formula: Optimal Hours = Base Hours x Experience Factor x Age Factor x Sleep Factor
rules.explanation: Base recovery by muscle group and intensity, adjusted for training experience, age, and sleep quality.

howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.q: How long should I rest between leg workouts?
faq.a: Legs (especially after heavy squats or deadlifts) need 48-72 hours minimum. Beginners may need up to 96 hours. Legs have larger muscles that generate more damage and require longer to fully repair.

faq.q: Can I work out a sore muscle?
faq.a: Mild soreness (DOMS) is normal and light activity can help. But training a muscle that is still significantly sore can lead to overtraining and injury. Wait until soreness drops to about 20% before training that muscle again at high intensity.
